text.heading: Fashola Calls for Media Partnership with Three Estates of the Realm
text.p: Lagos State Governor Babatunde Fashola has asked for a dynamic partnership between between the media and the other three estates of the realm in order to avoid animosity in governance.

Speaking at the biennial convention of the National Guild of Editors at the Ikeja Airport Hotel, Governor Fashola said in the absence of such partnership, the government would not function effectively.

Fashola, who was presented with two plagues ew: wrong choice of words - means "honour; award" etc. by Vanguard Newspaper Publisher Sam Pemu for his friendship with the Guild and achievements so far in the State, said that with the media as fourth estate, working with the goverment would enable a flow of right information to the public and consequently the sustainable development of the State.

According to him, the secret behind countries like South Africa maintaining an excellent international image despite negative happenings was a function of their media which plays down such incidences.
